---
title: "AB Testing is an Optimization Problem"
subtitle: "Why Traditional Stopping Rules Are Arbitrary"
author: "Russ Zaliznyak"
date: "2026-05-22"
categories: [AB Testing, Bayesian, Decision Theory, Optimization]
page-layout: full
jupyter: py311
execute:
  echo: false
css: wider.css
---

::: {.post-meta}
[In Progress]{.badge-wip}
:::

Traditional stopping rules use p-values to control error rates — specifically, the probability of falsely claiming a winner (Type I error). This is statistically sound, but it answers the wrong question.

Instead, we need to ask: what stopping rule maximizes our long-term outcomes?

# What Drives Stopping Criteria?

In order to determine optimal stopping rules, we're going to simulate 10,000 experiments like the one below:

```{=html}
<div class="stopping-factors-ui mt-3 mb-4">

  <!-- Buttons with inputs always visible below each -->
  <div class="factor-columns">

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn btn-outline-primary factor-btn active w-100" data-target="baseline">
        Baseline
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Conversion</label>
        <input type="text" class="form-control" value="65%" disabled>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn btn-outline-primary factor-btn w-100" data-target="traffic">
        Test Traffic &amp; Split
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <div class="d-flex gap-2">
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Traffic</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="100,000" disabled>
          </div>
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Split</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="50 / 50" disabled>
          </div>
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Min / Max Days</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="7 / 20" disabled>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn btn-outline-primary factor-btn w-100" data-target="posttest">
        Post-Test Traffic
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Post-Test Traffic</label>
        <input type="text" class="form-control" value="500,000" disabled>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn btn-outline-primary factor-btn w-100" data-target="effects">
        Expected Effects
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Experiment Type</label>
        <select class="form-select">
          <option disabled>Typical</option>
          <option disabled>Bold</option>
          <option selected>Transformative</option>
        </select>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <!-- Definition box -->
  <div class="factor-content p-3 border rounded mt-3" id="factor-display">
    <div class="factor-panel" id="panel-baseline">
      The pre-experiment conversion rate. Sets the variance of your test statistic
      and determines how much room for improvement exists.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-traffic">
      Total users in the experiment and their control/treatment split.
      More traffic means faster evidence. Uneven splits trade speed for risk protection.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-posttest">
      How long the winning variant runs after the decision. A longer deployment
      horizon means more value at stake — justifying a more aggressive stopping rule.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-effects">
      The realistic lift you expect comes from a <em>distribution</em> of possible effects — not a single number. Larger effects are detectable faster; smaller or more uncertain effects demand more traffic or more tolerance for risk.
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="mt-3">
  </div>

</div>



<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const simCollapse = document.getElementById('sim-collapse');
  const simHeader   = document.getElementById('sim-accordion-header');
  const simBtn      = document.getElementById('sim-accordion-btn');
  simCollapse.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse',  () => { simHeader.style.borderBottom = 'none'; simHeader.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; simBtn.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of Control'; });
  simCollapse.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse',  () => { simHeader.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; simHeader.style.borderRadius = '6px'; simBtn.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of Control'; });
});
</script>

<script>
document.querySelectorAll('.factor-btn').forEach(btn => {
  btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    document.querySelectorAll('.factor-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
    document.querySelectorAll('.factor-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.add('d-none'));
    btn.classList.add('active');
    document.getElementById('panel-' + btn.dataset.target).classList.remove('d-none');
  });
});
</script>
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Experiment Assumptions"

import simulationengine as se

# ── Experiment Assumptions ─────────────────────────────────────────
CONTROL_MEAN       = 0.65      # pre-experiment conversion rate
MAX_TEST_N         = 100_000   # total users in the experiment
SPLIT              = 0.50      # treatment allocation (50/50)
NUMBER_TEST_DAYS   = 20        # maximum experiment duration
POST_TEST_N        = 500_000   # users exposed after decision is made
NUMBER_SIMULATIONS = 10_000    # simulations to run
SEED               = 42
ITC_MEAN           = 100       # income-to-control mean
ITC_SE_TYPICAL     = 0.25      # income-to-control standard error (typical)
ITC_SE_TRANSFORMATIVE = 1      # income-to-control standard error (transformative)

df = se.create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    number_simulations=NUMBER_SIMULATIONS,
    seed=SEED,
    condition_parameters=[
        {
            "mean": CONTROL_MEAN,
            "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
        },
    ],
)

random_effect_parameters_0 = {
    "itc_mean": 100,
    "itc_se": ITC_SE_TRANSFORMATIVE,
    "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
}

df = se.add_random_effect_condition(df, random_effect_parameters_0)
df = se.add_cumulative_columns(df)
df = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(df)
df = se.compute_delta_stats(df)
df = se.compute_test_stats(df)

```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="sim-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="sim-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="sim-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:44px; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0; padding-bottom:0;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#sim-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Show Random Walks of Control</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="sim-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Simulation Charts"

import json
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
from IPython.display import display as ipy_display

# ── Time series data (first 100 sims, cumulative conversion rate) ─
N_SIMS = 100
plot_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("simulation") <= N_SIMS)
    .sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_0_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)

# ── Serialize all 100 sims for JS slider ─────────────────────────
sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = plot_df.filter(plot_df["simulation"] == sim)
    sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x": sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "y": [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["conversion_rate"].to_list()],
    }

# ── Pre-compute histogram bins for all 10k sims × 20 days ────────
all_df = (
    df.sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_0_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)
hist_data = {}
global_min = float(all_df["conversion_rate"].min())
global_max = float(all_df["conversion_rate"].max())

for day in range(1, NUMBER_TEST_DAYS + 1):
    rates = all_df.filter(all_df["day"] == day)["conversion_rate"].to_numpy()
    counts, edges = np.histogram(rates, bins=50)
    bin_centers = [float((edges[i] + edges[i+1]) / 2) for i in range(len(counts))]
    hist_data[str(day)] = {"x": bin_centers, "y": counts.tolist()}

# ── Subplot: time series (left) + histogram (right) ──────────────
fig = make_subplots(
    cols=2,
    column_widths=[0.5, 0.5],
    horizontal_spacing=0.08,
)

# Manually add left-aligned titles
fig.add_annotation(
    text="Each simulation takes its own path!",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.0, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)
fig.add_annotation(
    text="Control Estimates Improve Over Time (Day = 1)",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.54, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)

# Single trace — JS swaps x/y on slider change (trace index 0)
s1 = plot_df.filter(plot_df["simulation"] == 1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1["day"].to_list(),
    y=s1["conversion_rate"].to_list(),
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.9)", width=3),
    opacity=1.0,
    showlegend=False,
    name="Sim 1",
    hovertemplate="%{fullData.name} · %{y:.2%}<extra></extra>",
), col=1, row=1)

fig.add_hline(
    y=CONTROL_MEAN, line_dash="dash",
    line_color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)",
    annotation_text=f"True mean ({CONTROL_MEAN:.0%})",
    annotation_position="bottom right",
    col=1, row=1,
)

d1 = hist_data["1"]
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=d1["x"], y=d1["y"],
    marker_color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.55)",
    showlegend=False,
    hovertemplate="Rate: %{x:.2%}<br>Count: %{y}<extra></extra>",
), col=2, row=1)

fig.add_vline(
    x=CONTROL_MEAN, line_dash="dash",
    line_color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)",
    annotation_text=f"{CONTROL_MEAN:.0%}",
    annotation_position="top right",
    col=2, row=1,
)

fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=300,
    autosize=True,
    margin=dict(t=45, b=30, l=50, r=20),
    hovermode="closest",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="white", font_size=12, namelength=0),
)
fig.update_xaxes(tickformat="d", title_text="Day", col=1, row=1)
fig.update_xaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                 range=[global_min, global_max], col=2, row=1)
ts_min = float(plot_df["conversion_rate"].min())
ts_max = float(plot_df["conversion_rate"].max())
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                 range=[ts_min, ts_max], col=1, row=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="", showticklabels=False, col=2, row=1)

fig

```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(f'<script>window.__histData = {json.dumps(hist_data)}; window.__simData = {json.dumps(sim_data)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">

  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Day: <span id="day-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="day-slider" type="range" min="1" max="20" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>

</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attach() {
    const plot = document.querySelector('#sim-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!plot || !plot._fullData) { setTimeout(attach, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(plot);

    const simSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
    const simLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
    const daySlider = document.getElementById('day-slider');
    const dayLabel  = document.getElementById('day-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__simData[String(simNum)];
      Plotly.restyle(plot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y], name: [`Sim ${simNum}`] }, [0]);
    }

    simSlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      simLabel.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const tSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
      const tLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');
      if (tSlider && tSlider.value !== this.value) {
        tSlider.value = this.value;
        if (tLabel) tLabel.textContent = this.value;
        tSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const pvSlider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
      const pvLabel  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');
      if (pvSlider && pvSlider.value !== this.value) {
        pvSlider.value = this.value;
        if (pvLabel) pvLabel.textContent = this.value;
        pvSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    daySlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      const day = this.value;
      dayLabel.textContent = day;
      const d = window.__histData[day];
      Plotly.restyle(plot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y] }, [1]);
      Plotly.relayout(plot, { 'annotations[1].text': `Control Estimates Improve Over Time (Day = ${day})` });
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const collapse = document.getElementById('sim-collapse');
  if (collapse) {
    collapse.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attach);
  } else {
    attach();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="treatment-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="treatment-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="treatment-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:44px; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0; padding-bottom:0;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#treatment-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Show Random Walks of Treatment</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="treatment-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
# ── Treatment time series (first 100 sims) ───────────────────────
treat_plot_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("simulation") <= N_SIMS)
    .sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_1_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)

treat_true_mean = float(df["condition_1_true_mean"].mean())

treat_sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == sim)
    treat_sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x": sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "y": [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["conversion_rate"].to_list()],
        "true_mean": round(float(sim_df["condition_1_true_mean"].mean()), 6),
    }

# ── ITC histogram (last day, one value per simulation) ────────────
itc_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("day") == NUMBER_TEST_DAYS)
    .with_columns(
        (100 * pl.col("condition_1_true_mean") / pl.col("condition_0_true_mean")).alias("itc")
    )
    .sort("simulation")
)
itc_values = itc_df["itc"].to_numpy()
itc_counts, itc_edges = np.histogram(itc_values, bins=50)
itc_centers = [float((itc_edges[i] + itc_edges[i+1]) / 2) for i in range(len(itc_counts))]
itc_hist = {"x": itc_centers, "y": itc_counts.tolist()}
itc_max_count = int(itc_counts.max())

itc_max_dev = max(abs(float(itc_values.max()) - 100), abs(100 - float(itc_values.min())))
itc_range = [100 - itc_max_dev * 1.1, 100 + itc_max_dev * 1.1]

treat_itc_per_sim = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    rows = itc_df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == sim)["itc"]
    treat_itc_per_sim[str(sim)] = round(float(rows[0]), 4)

# ── Subplot figure ────────────────────────────────────────────────
t_fig = make_subplots(cols=2, column_widths=[0.5, 0.5], horizontal_spacing=0.08)

ts1 = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == 1)
s1_true_mean = round(float(ts1["condition_1_true_mean"].mean()), 6)

t_fig.add_annotation(
    text="Each simulation has a different true mean!",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.0, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)
t_fig.add_annotation(
    text="Distribution of True Treatment Effects (ITC)",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.54, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)

# annotation[2]: per-sim true effect label on time series line
t_fig.add_annotation(
    x=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    y=s1_true_mean,
    xref="x1", yref="y1",
    text=f"True mean ({s1_true_mean:.2%})",
    showarrow=False,
    xanchor="right",
    yanchor="top",
    font=dict(size=12, color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)"),
)

ts1 = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == 1)

t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=ts1["day"].to_list(),
    y=ts1["conversion_rate"].to_list(),
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.9)", width=3),
    opacity=1.0,
    showlegend=False,
    name="Sim 1",
    hovertemplate="%{fullData.name} · %{y:.2%}<extra></extra>",
), col=1, row=1)

# Trace 2: per-sim true mean line on time series
t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[1, NUMBER_TEST_DAYS],
    y=[s1_true_mean, s1_true_mean],
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)", width=1.5, dash="dash"),
    showlegend=False,
    hoverinfo="skip",
    name="True Mean",
), col=1, row=1)

# Trace 2: ITC histogram (fixed across all sims)
t_fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=itc_hist["x"], y=itc_hist["y"],
    marker_color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.45)",
    showlegend=False,
    hovertemplate="ITC: %{x:.1f}<br>Count: %{y}<extra></extra>",
), col=2, row=1)

# annotation[3]: ITC value label above the green dot
s1_itc = treat_itc_per_sim["1"]
s1_color = "green" if s1_itc >= 100 else "red"
t_fig.add_annotation(
    x=s1_itc,
    y=itc_max_count * 0.1,
    xref="x2", yref="y2",
    text=f"True Effect: {s1_itc:.2f}",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=2,
    arrowsize=2,
    arrowwidth=2,
    arrowcolor=s1_color,
    ax=0, ay=-40,
    font=dict(size=14, color=s1_color),
    bgcolor="white",
    bordercolor=s1_color,
    borderwidth=1,
)
t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[s1_itc],
    y=[0],
    mode="markers",
    marker=dict(color=s1_color, size=12, symbol="circle"),
    showlegend=False,
    hoverinfo="skip",
    name="Selected Sim",
), col=2, row=1)

t_fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=300,
    autosize=True,
    margin=dict(t=45, b=30, l=50, r=20),
    hovermode="closest",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="white", font_size=12, namelength=0),
)
t_fig.update_xaxes(tickformat="d", title_text="Day", col=1, row=1)
t_fig.update_xaxes(tickformat=".1f", title_text="Index-to-Control (ITC)",
                   range=itc_range, col=2, row=1)
t_treat_min = float(treat_plot_df["conversion_rate"].min())
t_treat_max = float(treat_plot_df["conversion_rate"].max())
t_fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                   range=[t_treat_min, t_treat_max], col=1, row=1)
t_fig.update_yaxes(title_text="", showticklabels=False, col=2, row=1)

t_fig
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(f'<script>window.__treatSimData = {json.dumps(treat_sim_data)}; window.__treatItcPerSim = {json.dumps(treat_itc_per_sim)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">
  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="t-sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="t-sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attachTreatment() {
    const tplot = document.querySelector('#treatment-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!tplot || !tplot._fullData) { setTimeout(attachTreatment, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(tplot);

    const simSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
    const simLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__treatSimData[String(simNum)];
      const itc = window.__treatItcPerSim[String(simNum)];
      const pct = (d.true_mean * 100).toFixed(2) + '%';
      const color = itc >= 100 ? 'green' : 'red';
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y], name: [`Sim ${simNum}`] }, [0]);
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { y: [[d.true_mean, d.true_mean]] }, [1]);
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { x: [[itc]], 'marker.color': [color] }, [3]);
      Plotly.relayout(tplot, {
        'annotations[2].y': d.true_mean,
        'annotations[2].text': `True mean (${pct})`,
        'annotations[3].x': itc,
        'annotations[3].text': `True Effect: ${itc.toFixed(2)}`,
        'annotations[3].font.color': color,
        'annotations[3].arrowcolor': color,
        'annotations[3].bordercolor': color,
      });
    }

    simSlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      simLabel.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const cSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
      const cLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
      if (cSlider && cSlider.value !== this.value) {
        cSlider.value = this.value;
        if (cLabel) cLabel.textContent = this.value;
        cSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const pvSlider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
      const pvLabel  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');
      if (pvSlider && pvSlider.value !== this.value) {
        pvSlider.value = this.value;
        if (pvLabel) pvLabel.textContent = this.value;
        pvSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const tc = document.getElementById('treatment-collapse');
  if (tc) {
    tc.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attachTreatment);
  } else {
    attachTreatment();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const tc = document.getElementById('treatment-collapse');
  const th = document.getElementById('treatment-accordion-header');
  const tb = document.getElementById('treatment-accordion-btn');
  tc.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse', () => { th.style.borderBottom = 'none'; th.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; tb.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of Treatment'; });
  tc.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse', () => { th.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; th.style.borderRadius = '6px'; tb.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of Treatment'; });
});
</script>
```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="pvalue-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="pvalue-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="pvalue-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:44px; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0; padding-bottom:0;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#pvalue-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1; white-space:nowrap; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis;">Show Random Walks of P-Values</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="pvalue-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

s1_pv_df = df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == 1).sort("day")

pv_fig = go.Figure()

pv_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1_pv_df["day"].to_list(),
    y=[round(1 - v, 6) for v in s1_pv_df["condition_1_pr_positive_diff"].to_list()],
    mode="lines",
    name="Treatment",
    line=dict(color="#e45c3a", width=2),
    hovertemplate="p = %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="#c03a1e", font_color="white", bordercolor="#c03a1e", font_size=14),
))
pv_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1_pv_df["day"].to_list(),
    y=[round(1 - v, 6) for v in s1_pv_df["condition_0_pr_highest_diff"].to_list()],
    mode="lines",
    name="Control",
    line=dict(color="#1e46a0", width=2),
    hovertemplate="p = %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    hoverlabel=dict(font_size=14),
))
pv_fig.add_hline(
    y=0.05,
    line_dash="dash",
    line_color="gray",
    opacity=0.6,
    annotation_text="p = 0.05",
    annotation_position="right",
)
pv_fig.update_layout(
    height=320,
    yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1.05], title="P-Value", tickformat=".3f"),
    xaxis=dict(title="Day"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.01, xanchor="left", x=0),
    margin=dict(t=40, b=40, l=60, r=80),
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
pv_fig.update_yaxes(gridcolor="#eeeeee")
pv_fig.update_xaxes(gridcolor="#eeeeee")

pv_fig
```

```{python}
#| output: asis

pval_sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == sim).sort("day")
    pval_sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x":       sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "treat":   [round(1 - v, 6) for v in sim_df["condition_1_pr_positive_diff"].to_list()],
        "control": [round(1 - v, 6) for v in sim_df["condition_0_pr_highest_diff"].to_list()],
    }

print(f'<script>window.__pvalSimData = {json.dumps(pval_sim_data)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">
  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="pval-sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="pval-sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attachPval() {
    const pvplot = document.querySelector('#pvalue-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!pvplot || !pvplot._fullData) { setTimeout(attachPval, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(pvplot);

    const slider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
    const label  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__pvalSimData[String(simNum)];
      Plotly.restyle(pvplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.treat]   }, [0]);
      Plotly.restyle(pvplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.control] }, [1]);
    }

    slider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      label.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const cSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
      const cLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
      if (cSlider && cSlider.value !== this.value) {
        cSlider.value = this.value;
        if (cLabel) cLabel.textContent = this.value;
        cSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const tSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
      const tLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');
      if (tSlider && tSlider.value !== this.value) {
        tSlider.value = this.value;
        if (tLabel) tLabel.textContent = this.value;
        tSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const pc2 = document.getElementById('pvalue-collapse');
  if (pc2) {
    pc2.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attachPval);
    const ph = document.getElementById('pvalue-accordion-header');
    const pb2 = document.getElementById('pvalue-accordion-btn');
    pc2.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse', () => { ph.style.borderBottom = 'none'; ph.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; pb2.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of P-Values'; });
    pc2.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse', () => { ph.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; ph.style.borderRadius = '6px'; pb2.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of P-Values'; });
  } else {
    attachPval();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="alert mt-3" style="background:#f8f9fa; border-left: 4px solid #1e46a0; border-radius:4px; padding: 0.85rem 1.1rem; font-size:0.9rem; color:#333;">
  <strong>Control:</strong> Each simulation is a different realization of the same baseline, but different noise. This is the randomness inherent in experimentation.<br><br>
  <strong>Treatment:</strong> Each simulation of Treatment has a different true effect. Sometimes harmful, sometimes beneficial, often marginal. Even when we collect test data, we never know the exact truth.
</div>
```

# Determine Optimal Stopping Rule

With a strategy.
